mentions.note: Down 8 straight weeks; other side of storage shortage trade — needs to pay sky-high storage prices, margin pressure. Own it, don't trade it; if guides margins down, decline may not be over.

Gold miner; best miner to participate in gold shortage (only 1% of world holdings replaced annually). Gold up 15% YTD, silver up 46% on dollar weakness and geopolitical uncertainty. Own gold long-term, not crypto (lacks shortage factor for inflation hedge).
Semiconductor capital equipment maker; hit today on Intel pin action but I think mistaken — industry in great shape, just Intel's execution problem.
Chip equipment supplier gaining on shortage tailwinds; Intel miss provides opportunity.
Rallied nicely as they DID factor shortage into guidance; wish we hadn't sold for Charitable Trust. Beneficiary of Intel's execution stumble.
Rallied nicely as Intel stumbled; better factored CPU shortage into guidance. Regret not holding for Trust.
Rallied on news of big planned layoffs post-Intel; money flowing back to Mag 7 from shortage plays.
Very interesting; Renee Huang (CEO) got shortage coming right; great buy. CEO bet validated by chip shortage dynamics.
Reports Friday; almost always good quarter, stock almost always hit (pattern observed). Stupid selling — perfect buying opportunity.

Gary asks about breakdown after years as darling; mystifies me. Best buyback (shrunk float 50% in years); maybe tariff parts concern but little downside here after recent weakness. Can get back in now.
Had monster move post-accident recovery; don't sell as multi-year turnaround play is early innings. Stock at pre-Alaska Airlines accident levels.
AI-native corporate card/expense management platform acquired by Capital One for $5.15B; strategic move to compete with American Express in corporate segment.
Up 60% last year; recent urology acquisition impressive. Cramer targets $300; sees upside from healthcare consolidation.
$48B healthcare services/products company; up 60% last year. I'm most blown away with (over pharma companies); urology purchase just made shows growth trajectory. See $300 target.
Data center backup generator play; used to dip on earnings, now rallies because people can't believe consistent strength. Still rallying, wrong not to like it.
Stock has big run; trim on Monday if concerned about Google backing out of potential deal. Better risk/reward after weakness.
May be overbought; Google backing out concern presents risk. Trim Monday one day — big run already, take profits.

Chris asks when to buy Caterpillar after huge run; prefer Cummins here as better buy. CAT down today but reports next week; people fear data center stall but CMI represents better entry after CAT's large move.

Q4 miss on reserve charges but credit metrics solid (charge-offs down); Brex $5.15B fintech acquisition strategic (take on Amex corporate card). Down 16% from highs at 11x earnings vs Amex 21x — at 16x multiple worth $330. Building full-service financial powerhouse.

Mixed quarter (revenue beat, EPS miss) and $5B Brex acquisition caused 16% decline. Executing on full-service financial services strategy; valuation at 11x earnings offers opportunity if closes gap to 16x.
Transports hottest group; new CEO Steve Angel rebuilding company with cost savings; low single-digit revenue growth, 200-300 bps margin expansion guidance.
Throws off massive cash, big buyback, 4% yield. My favorite for very long time for consistency. Venezuela kicker as they operate there and know what needs doing.
Merger with Capital One created credit card powerhouse; integration proceeding as planned.
After dry spell, getting big biotech orders; better managed quarters recently; stock down today could be opportunity. First truly strong quarter in years possible.
Coming off dry spell; big biotech orders for expensive equipment may drive strong quarter. Stock down today; possible opportunity.
Leslie asks buy/sell/hold; reports Jan 29. Jumped 5 pts day before, we took profit; down today. CEO Rich Tobin doing terrific job but wait for quarter before opining. Don't have enough info yet.
Caller holds and won't sell due to AI infrastructure play. Centerpiece of AI buildout but already massively run up from $140 to $657.
Spun from GE April 2024 at $140, now $657; power division has more business than can handle from data centers. Love long-term but expectations sky-high — need better entry point.
Up 15% YoY on shortage, dollar weakness, geopolitical uncertainty. Own gold, don't trade it.
Replacing copper with fiber in data centers nationwide. Prospects terrific — own the business. Should hope it comes down to buy; I wasn't bullish enough in club meeting.
Reports Tuesday; stock tends to underperform first 1-2 hours (pattern observed); CEO Mary Barra deserves more respect for delivery. Pull trigger on dip.

Stock goes down almost every time reports; planning to list quantum business Quintessium but breaking into pieces complicates story. Traders will reject automation/aerospace splits — expect post-report hit.
Last quarter reported number that left street wrangled but stock quickly recovered. Could do same this time as traders dominate. Amazing company, don't understand why stock acted this way.

Down 17% on Q1 sub-optimal guidance due to inability to produce CPUs fast enough despite massive shortage; execution miss not demand problem. Still great company under Lip Buu; will take advantage of CPU shortage.
Missed guidance on CPU shortage (supply constraint, not demand); down 17% but still great company under Lip Boutan. Will benefit from CPU shortage despite poor execution this quarter.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker; hit today on Intel pin action but I think mistaken — industry in great shape, just Intel's execution problem.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker in shortage game; hold through Intel volatility.
Reports Tuesday morning; expect better quarter than P&G; merging with Kleenex in cash-stock deal but warming up to stock after P&G and Clorox rallies.
Bob asks about defense contractors with reasonable dividend and long-term appeal. I like Lockheed Martin; always confused by Trump's no-buyback/no-dividend threat but still works here.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker; hit today on Intel pin action but I think mistaken — industry in great shape, just Intel's execution problem.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker benefiting from memory/storage shortage; guilt-by-association selloff with Intel unjustified.
Terrible until today; nice move yesterday, recommended last 2 days. Mark Zuckerberg lowered boom on spending all capex can; explain how making money on data center buildout beyond smart glasses.
Weak partly because all software under pressure from generative AI concern (false worry in MSFT case); shaved P/E multiple. Very difficult to own except select days.
Memory/storage play; endless buying in last year due to data center shortage surge. Shortage-driven rally continues.
Memory chip maker in short supply; data center demand driving shortages.
Caller Jude asks about overvaluation risk; Mag Seven stocks overvalued and when one slows spending, Nebus cut in half. Don't want that to happen.
Robert asks if 2026 will see recovery after stock split pain. Conference call this week was show of force — Netflix completely on its game. Buyer here.
Alongside Salesforce, SaaS leaders; both doing well but stocks not playing ball. NOW down 100+ pts from high. Bill McDermott must explain if broken stock vs broken company.
Selected for Union Pacific merger; major beneficiary of regulatory clarity under Trump administration.

Digital bank operating Latin America (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia); US bank charter approved; upgraded by Goldman/Morgan Stanley/BofA. Like it but P/E too high even for forward-looking bank; prefer Santander.
Best steel company in world; pre-announced strong earnings, up 12% since; buy on any Monday dip given Fed rate cuts and tariffs.
Rallied on rumor Chinese government allowing H200 chip sales to ByteDance, Tencent, Alibaba. Story's veracity uncertain.
Richard asks about chip cyclicality; NXPI is cyclical on semi-cyclical. Can't buy cyclical chips (have to buy then sell). Prefer to hold secular chip plays instead.
Cramer selling, not buying. Hype tech company but not commercial enterprise. Compare unfavorably to GE Vernova which knows how to build/operate.
Rallied on what many thought was bad quarter; provides positive precedent for similar companies.

Bob asks about defense contractors with reasonable dividend and long-term appeal. I like RTX (aerospace/defense environment); liked since 1978. Trump's no-buyback/no-dividend threat makes hornet's nest for many.
International bank alternative to Nubank; offered as superior option for those seeking international bank exposure.
Wildly overbought; reports Wednesday then investor day Thursday (structure confusing). Can't tell good enough story to justify recent ramp unless insanely strong numbers.
Up 46% YoY but world has plenty of silver unlike gold. Dollar weakness and inflation hedging primary drivers.
Up huge already, way too much for Cramer. But storage shortage real; positive reception expected.
Storage and memory chip maker benefiting from massive data center demand surge; up 25% YTD but needs massive beat-and-raise to maintain momentum. Likely to deliver.
Repeat: Tesla is robot company and autonomous driving play, not just car company. This quarter could define new narrative — if so, stock skyrockets from earnings announcement.
Richard asks about chip cyclicality; TXN is cyclical. Can't buy cyclical chips (have to buy then sell). Prefer to hold secular chip plays instead.
Attempting to merge with Norfolk Southern creating transcontinental railroad; Trump admin lazy-fair approach supportive.
Storage play reporting same time as Apple; will be very positive. Won't get same reception as Apple — 'Wow' momentum buying continues despite huge run.
Michelle asks which businesses benefit from AI beyond chips/energy/infra. Banks will benefit most from AI efficiency; Wells Fargo has best handle on it — we own for Charitable Trust.
Throws off massive cash; reports Friday alongside Chevron.
